prompt.system:
You are a legal research assistant specialising in formal methods
applied to law. Your answers must be structured to support formalisation in Lean 4
dependent type theory. You are helping build a library called LegalLean that encodes
legal reasoning with machine-checkable guarantees.

CRITICAL: Structure your response as JSON with the following schema. Do NOT return
prose — return structured data that maps to type definitions.

Be exhaustive about conditions, exceptions, and vagueness. Legal formalisation
requires knowing EVERY rule, exception, and point of interpretive discretion.
prompt.user:
Analyse the following legal provision and return a JSON object
with this exact structure:

{
  "provision": {
    "id": "string — official citation",
    "title": "string — short name",
    "jurisdiction": "string",
    "source_url": "string — authoritative source URL"
  },
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "string — unique rule identifier",
      "description": "string — what this rule establishes",
      "modality": "permitted | prohibited | obligatory",
      "conditions": [
        {
          "id": "string",
          "description": "string — natural language condition",
          "formalisable": true/false,
          "if_not_formalisable": "string — why (vague term? discretionary?)"
        }
      ],
      "exceptions": [
        {
          "id": "string",
          "description": "string — what defeats this rule",
          "priority": "string — why this exception wins (lex specialis, lex posterior, etc.)",
          "source": "string — statutory reference for the exception"
        }
      ],
      "definitions": [
        {
          "term": "string — defined term",
          "definition": "string — statutory definition",
          "has_open_texture": true/false,
          "core_meaning": "string — settled, uncontested meaning",
          "penumbra": "string — contested, unclear boundary cases"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "defeat_relations": [
    {
      "defeater_rule": "string — rule ID that defeats",
      "defeated_rule": "string — rule ID that is defeated",
      "reason": "string — why (lex specialis, lex posterior, lex superior, etc.)",
      "strict": true/false
    }
  ],
  "discretionary_checkpoints": [
    {
      "id": "string",
      "authority": "string — who decides",
      "decision": "string — what they decide",
      "constraints": ["string — limits on their discretion"],
      "gated_rule": "string — which rule this gates"
    }
  ],
  "open_texture_terms": [
    {
      "term": "string",
      "type": "vague | discretionary",
      "core": "string — clear cases",
      "penumbra": "string — unclear cases",
      "authority": "string — who resolves ambiguity",
      "case_law": ["string — key cases interpreting this term"]
    }
  ],
  "key_references": [
    {
      "author": "string",
      "title": "string",
      "year": "number",
      "relevance": "string",
      "url": "string"
    }
  ]
}

Legal provision to analyse:
O-1 Visa Extraordinary Ability Criteria

Additional context:
This is for the LegalLean monograph. We need to formalise this provision in Lean 4 dependent type theory.

The stub is in LegalLean/CaseStudies/O1Visa.lean at line 23.
Target LegalLean types: DefeasiblyHolds, FormalisationBoundary, EvidenceFor.

Code context:
  - Formalisable elements (document counts, membership lists)
  - Open texture requiring USCIS adjudicator discretion ("sustained national
    or international acclaim", "extraordinary ability", "comparable evidence")
-/

import LegalLean.Core
import LegalLean.Defeasible

namespace LegalLean.CaseStudies.O1Visa

-- TODO: Formalise O-1A eligibility criteria as dependent types
-- Each criterion: a proposition that can be evidenced
-- Eligibility: ∃ (s : Finset (Fin 8)), s.card ≥ 3 ∧ ∀ i ∈ s, EvidenceFor (criterion i)
-- FormalisationBoundary: mark "extraordinary ability" and "comparable evidence"
-- as Discretionary (USCIS adjudicator)

Be especially thorough about:
1. Every condition and sub-condition for the rule to apply
2. Every exception that can defeat the rule
3. Every term with open texture (vagueness or discretion)
4. The defeat priority ordering between rules
5. Who has discretion and what constrains it

rules.description: Individual is eligible for O-1A visa if they have extraordinary ability in sciences, education, business, or athletics and meet at least 3 of 8 specific criteria

conditions.description: Has extraordinary ability in sciences, education, business, or athletics (demonstrated by sustained national or international acclaim)
conditions.if_not_formalisable: Open texture terms 'extraordinary ability' and 'sustained national or international acclaim' require USCIS adjudicator discretion

conditions.description: Meets at least 3 of the 8 evidentiary criteria listed in 8 CFR § 204.5(h)(3)

definitions.definition: Degree of expertise significantly above that ordinarily encountered, by sustained national or international acclaim [8 CFR § 204.5(h)(2)]
definitions.core_meaning: Top field experts with major awards, consistent high-level achievements
definitions.penumbra: Boundary cases between 'very good' vs 'extraordinary'; relative comparisons

definitions.definition: Continued recognition over time at national or international level
definitions.core_meaning: Multi-year recognition through awards, publications, media coverage
definitions.penumbra: Duration required? National vs international threshold? Recent vs historical acclaim?

rules.description: Receipt of nationally or internationally recognized prize or award for excellence in field

conditions.description: Award is nationally or internationally recognized for excellence
conditions.if_not_formalisable: 'Recognized' and 'excellence' require adjudicator judgment of prestige level

rules.description: Membership in associations requiring outstanding achievement (judged by experts)

conditions.description: Association requires outstanding achievement as judged by recognized experts
conditions.if_not_formalisable: Discretionary judgment of what constitutes 'outstanding achievement'

rules.description: Published material in professional or major media about the person's work

conditions.description: Published material exists about the person's work in field
Material appears in professional or major trade publications or media

rules.description: Participation as judge of others' work individually or on panel

conditions.description: Has participated as judge of work of others in field

rules.description: Original scientific, scholarly, or business-related contributions of major significance

conditions.description: Contributions have major significance in field (evidenced by experts)

rules.description: Authorship of scholarly articles in professional journals or major media

conditions.description: Articles published in professional journals or major media

rules.description: Employment in critical or essential capacity for distinguished organizations
Commanded high salary or other remuneration indicating extraordinary ability
Comparable evidence may be submitted if traditional criteria do not apply

conditions.description: Traditional evidentiary criteria do not readily apply to field
conditions.if_not_formalisable: USCIS discretion to determine applicability and sufficiency
Complete adjudicator discretion on what constitutes 'comparable'

definitions.definition: Evidence comparable in import, relevance, and weight to listed criteria
definitions.core_meaning: Direct substitutes for listed criteria (e.g., patents for publications)
definitions.penumbra: Novel evidence types; weight determination entirely discretionary

defeat_relations.reason: statutory alternative when traditional criteria do not apply (lex specialis)

discretionary_checkpoints.decision: Whether applicant has 'extraordinary ability' demonstrated by sustained acclaim
Whether submitted evidence satisfies at least 3 criteria
Whether comparable evidence demonstrates extraordinary ability
constraints: Must be comparable in weight to traditional criteria

open_texture_terms.core: Nobel Prize winners, Olympic medalists, field-leading researchers
open_texture_terms.penumbra: Very accomplished vs extraordinarily distinguished; field-relative standards

open_texture_terms.core: Consistent recognition over 3+ years via multiple channels
open_texture_terms.penumbra: Short-term fame vs sustained? National vs international threshold?

open_texture_terms.core: Direct substitutes: patents for publications, performances for awards
open_texture_terms.penumbra: Novel evidence types; weight equivalence determination
Regional vs national? Online vs print? Depth of coverage?

key_references.author: USCIS
key_references.title: O-1 Visa Individuals with Extraordinary Ability or Achievement
key_references.relevance: Official policy manual interpretation of criteria

key_references.author: Kazarian v. USCIS
key_references.title: Kazarian v. USCIS, 596 F.3d 1115 (9th Cir. 2010)
key_references.relevance: Two-part adjudicative framework: meet criteria, then final merits determination

consensus.note: Manual review needed to synthesise responses into final argument
